title: Gomphia lutambensis
taxonomy: Gomphia lutambensis is classified within the family Olacaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the sapindales order. Gomphia lutambensis is further classified into the genus Gomphia, which contains about 30 species of plants. The genus Gomphia is part of the order Santalales and is native to tropical regions of Africa. Gomphia lutambensis is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the class Magnoliopsida, which includes most of the flowering plants. Gomphia lutambensis is also classified as a type of tree or shrub that is commonly found in forests and woodlands.
uses: Gomphia lutambensis is known for its medicinal properties, which are primarily used in traditional African medicine.
